rationale: The citing paper uses Argo, Dahl, and Morales (2008) in its core conceptual framing of magical contagion, not merely in a background list. In the theory development, it relies on this citation to support the claim that a product-related residue can represent a source's essence only when consumers believe a credible source-product link exists, which feeds directly into its boundary-condition hypotheses about scent expectations and congruence. This role informs the mechanism and hypothesis setup of the scented-advertising model, so the citation is substantively foundational.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Argo et al. (2008) as conceptual evidence from positive contagion research to justify that transferred traces can carry an entity's essence when attribution is credible. It uses that logic as part of the theoretical foundation for predicting when scented ads increase perceived product proximity and appeal.
